prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo coiled-coil decoy protein (60–80 residues) that competitively inhibits HA33 binding to intestinal mucin glycans, thereby blocking oral absorption of botulinum neurotoxin complex as part of a medical countermeasure programme against botulism. The designed protein should achieve Ki < 500 nM for HA33 displacement from asialofetuin in a competitive ELISA. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone targeting the HA33 carbohydrate-binding groove (PDB 2INJ), (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design mimicking galactose-recognition geometry, (3) structure validation and binding assay design. Include a discussion of administration routes and bioavailability constraints.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (2INJ); target: BoNT complex HA33 hemagglutinin; organism: Clostridium botulinum; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: Main hemagglutinin component HA33 (botulinum complex type C)
_provenance.target_class: galactose-binding carbohydrate-recognition domain of BoNT progenitor complex (select agent toxin subunit; P0DPR0 is the Swiss-Prot reviewed representative of the HA33 family; type A structure used in PDB 2INJ is structurally equivalent)
